Class WorkBatch

java.lang.Object
com.github.mrgarbagegamer.WorkBatch
public final class WorkBatch extends Object
A high-performance, reusable container for batching puzzle combinations

This class is a cornerstone for the solver's performance architecture. It functions as a custom, fixed-size circular buffer that groups thousands of individual short[] puzzle combinations into a single object. This batching strategy is crucial for reducing contention on combination queues and minimizing synchronization overhead between generators and monkeys.

Architecture Role and Performance Impact

In high-performance computing, frequent queue operations can become a major bottleneck. This was the case in earlier versions of this solver, where generators enqueued millions of individual combinations, consuming 30-40% of CPU time in queue management alone.

WorkBatch fundamentally solves this problem by changing the unit of work transfer. Instead of individual arrays, generators now fill a WorkBatch and enqueue it once. Monkeys dequeue the entire batch and process its contents. This approach reduces the number of queue operations by several orders of magnitude (from millions to hundreds), effectively eliminating the queue as a bottleneck and dramatically improving cache locality.

Memory Management

To achieve near-zero garbage collection pressure in the hot-path, WorkBatch instances are designed to be reusable. The internal buffer is pre-allocated once at construction. After a monkey finishes processing a batch, the batch object is returned to the a central pool managed by CombinationQueueArray, making it available for generators to use again. This pooling strategy, combined with the pre-allocated buffer, nearly eliminates runtime memory allocation for combination data.

Thread Safety

This class is not thread-safe. An instance of WorkBatch must only be accessed by a single thread at a time. The architecture enforces this by design: a generator thread owns a batch while filling it, and a monkey owns it after dequeuing it. Queues are the sole mechanism for safely transferring ownership between threads.

Since:
2025.07 - WorkBatch Introduction
See Also:
  • ArrayPool
  • TaskPool
Performance Characteristics:
O(1) for poll operations and simple state checks, O(numClicks) for add operations due to array copying.
Threading Model:
This class is not thread-safe, as each instance of WorkBatch is intended to be used by one thread at a time. Queues should be the only point of inter-thread communication.
Memory Management:
Fixed memory usage based on the capacity and number of clicks, minimizing dynamic allocations.

  • Field Details

    • buffer

      private final short[][] buffer
      The pre-allocated circular buffer storing the short[] combinations.

      This buffer is the core of the WorkBatch. It is allocated once at construction and continuously reused to store combination data. This strategy is fundamental to the solver's low-allocation approach, as it avoids the immense garbage collection pressure that would result from creating new arrays for billions of combinations.

      Since:
      2025.07 - WorkBatch Introduction
      See Also:
      • WorkBatch(int)
      • add(short[])
      • poll()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) for poll operations, O(numClicks) for add operations due to array copying.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe; must be accessed by only one thread at a time.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of ~capacity × numClicks × 2 bytes as a short[][].

    • capacity

      private final int capacity
      The maximum number of combinations this batch can hold.

      Batch capacity is a critical tuning parameter. A larger capacity reduces the frequency of expensive queue operations but increases memory footprint and may introduce latency if batches take too long to fill. A smaller capacity has the opposite effect. The optimal value balances these trade-offs to maximize throughput.

      Since:
      2025.07 - WorkBatch Introduction
      See Also:
      • buffer
      • WorkBatch(int)
      • add(short[])
      • isFull()
      • CombinationGeneratorTask.BATCH_SIZE
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) field access.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe; must be accessed by only one thread at a time.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of 4 bytes as an int.

    • numClicks

      private static int numClicks
      The number of clicks (elements) in each combination array.

      This static value determines the size of the inner arrays within the buffer. It must be configured via setNumClicks(int) once at application startup before any WorkBatch instances are created. This ensures that all pre-allocated buffers have the correct dimensions for the target puzzle.

      Since:
      2025.07 - NPE in WorkBatch Operations Fix
      See Also:
      • buffer
      • add(short[], int, short)
      • setNumClicks(int)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) for access.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe after initialization.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of 4 bytes as an int.

    • head

      private int head
      The index of the next combination to be read from the circular buffer.

      This pointer advances when poll() is called and wraps around the buffer, enabling efficient, continuous reads without reallocating memory. Using separate head and tail integer pointers is a deliberate choice for performance, as it avoids the arithmetic overhead that a single-pointer implementation would require on every add/poll operation.

      Since:
      2025.07 - WorkBatch Introduction
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) for poll operations.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe; must be accessed by only one thread at a time.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of 4 bytes as an int.

    • tail

      private int tail
      The index of the next available slot for writing into the circular buffer.

      This pointer advances when an item is added via add(short[]) or add(short[], int, short) and wraps around the buffer. Using an int instead of a short avoids potential overflow issues and unusual JVM arithmetic handling for shorts, while the use of a separate tail pointer (from head) minimizes computational overhead in the hot path.

      Since:
      2025.07 - WorkBatch Introduction
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) for add operations.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe; must be accessed by only one thread at a time.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of 4 bytes as an int.

    • remainingCapacity

      private int remainingCapacity
      Tracks the number of available slots in the batch.

      This counter is used instead of a traditional size field. Checking against remaining capacity (e.g., remainingCapacity == 0) is often more favorable to JIT compiler optimizations and branch prediction than checking a variable size. It decrements on add and increments on poll.

      Since:
      2025.07 - Remaining Capacity Tracking
      See Also:
      • capacity
      • add(short[])
      • poll()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) updates and comparisons.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe; must be accessed by only one thread at a time.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of 4 bytes as an int.

  • Constructor Details

    • WorkBatch

      public WorkBatch(int capacity)
      Constructs a new WorkBatch with a pre-allocated internal buffer.

      The buffer size is determined by the specified capacity and the static numClicks value, which must be set via setNumClicks(int) before calling this constructor.

      Parameters:
      capacity - The maximum number of combinations the batch can hold.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if capacity is not a positive integer.
      IllegalStateException - if numClicks has not been set to a positive value.
      Since:
      2025.07 - WorkBatch Introduction
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(capacity × numClicks) for initial buffer allocation, O(1) for construction.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe during construction; the resulting instance is not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Allocates a short[capacity][numClicks].

  • Method Details

    • setNumClicks

      public static void setNumClicks(int numClicks)
      Sets the static number of clicks for all WorkBatch instances.

      This method must be called once during application initialization before any batches are created. It ensures all pre-allocated buffers have the correct dimensions for the puzzle being solved.

      Parameters:
      numClicks - The number of elements in each combination array.
      Since:
      2025.07 - Pre-allocation of WorkBatch Buffers
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) for field assignment.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe (at the moment); must be called once before any instances are created.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • add

      public boolean add(short[] source)
      Adds a combination to the batch by copying it into the internal buffer.

      This method is a hot path, optimized to be allocation-free. It uses System.arraycopy() to transfer the combination data into the pre-allocated buffer, avoiding the overhead of creating new array objects.

      Parameters:
      source - The combination array to add. The caller must ensure this is not null.
      Returns:
      true if the combination was added, or false if the batch is full.
      Since:
      2025.07 - WorkBatch Introduction
      See Also:
      • isFull()
      • setNumClicks(int)
      • CombinationGeneratorTask
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(numClicks) array copying, O(1) capacity checks and pointer updates.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe; must be accessed by only one thread at a time.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate; reuses the pre-allocated arrays in buffer.

    • add

      public final boolean add(short[] prefix, int prefixLength, short lastElement)
      Adds a combination by assembling it directly into the internal buffer from a prefix and a final element.

      This highly optimized method is a critical performance enhancement for the CombinationGeneratorTask. It allows the generator to construct the final combination at the leaf nodes of its recursion tree without allocating a temporary array. Instead, the prefix is copied and the last element is appended directly into the target slot in the buffer, eliminating an intermediate array copy and allocation.

      Parameters:
      prefix - The prefix of the combination. The caller must ensure this is not null.
      prefixLength - The length of the prefix to copy.
      lastElement - The final element to append to the combination.
      Returns:
      true if the combination was added, or false if the batch is full.
      Since:
      2025.07 - Assembling Combinations in the WorkBatch
      See Also:
      • isFull()
      • setNumClicks(int)
      • CombinationGeneratorTask
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) array access in buffer, O(prefixLength) array copy.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe; must be accessed by only one thread at a time.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate; reuses the pre-allocated arrays in buffer.

    • poll

      public short[] poll()
      Retrieves the next combination from the batch without allocating a new array.

      This method returns a direct reference to the array within the internal buffer. The returned array is not nulled out in the buffer and will be overwritten by subsequent add operations once the circular buffer wraps around. This is a key performance feature, but it means the caller must process the returned array before it is overwritten.

      Returns:
      The next combination array, or null if the batch is empty.
      Since:
      2025.07 - WorkBatch Introduction
      See Also:
      • buffer
      • add(short[], int, short)
      • clear()
      • isEmpty()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) for polling operations.
      Threading Model:
      This method is not thread-safe, as each instance of WorkBatch is intended to be used by one thread at a time.
      Memory Management:
      The memory footprint is fixed based on the capacity and numClicks, minimizing dynamic allocations. The method does not allocate any new arrays, instead reusing the pre-allocated arrays in buffer.

    • isEmpty

      public boolean isEmpty()
      Checks if the batch contains no combinations.
      Returns:
      true if the batch is empty, false otherwise.
      Since:
      2025.07 - WorkBatch Introduction
      See Also:
      • capacity
      • remainingCapacity
      • isFull()
      • size()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) retrievals and comparison.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe; must be accessed by only one thread at a time.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • size

      public int size()
      Returns the number of combinations currently stored in the batch.

      This is calculated from the capacity and remainingCapacity to avoid the potential for JIT deoptimizations associated with a separate, mutable size field.

      Returns:
      The number of combinations in the batch.
      Since:
      2025.07 - Remaining Capacity Tracking
      See Also:
      • isEmpty()
      • isFull()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) calculation.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe; must be accessed by only one thread at a time.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • clear

      public void clear()
      Resets the batch to an empty state, making it ready for reuse.

      This O(1) operation simply resets the internal pointers and counters. It does not modify the underlying buffer array, allowing the batch to be efficiently recycled without any deallocation or reallocation.

      Since:
      2025.07 - WorkBatch Introduction
      See Also:
      • WorkBatch(int)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) resets.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe; must be accessed by only one thread at a time.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate or deallocate.

    • isFull

      public boolean isFull()
      Checks if the batch has reached its maximum capacity.
      Returns:
      true if the batch is full, false otherwise.
      Since:
      2025.07 - Remaining Capacity Tracking
      See Also:
      • capacity
      • isEmpty()
      • size()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) retrievals and comparison.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe; must be accessed by only one thread at a time.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

Performance-optimized brute-force solver for hexagonal grid Lights Out puzzles
